item.title: FT High-Growth Companies Asia-Pacific
item.description: The 2026 ranking of fast growing companies in the Asia-Pacific region. Plus: Indian winemakers face change; Chinese AI experts return from US; the biggest threat to China’s champions; ticket fees donated to charities; Singapore listings remain scarce

item.title: Chinese and Indian airports chase hub status amid Iran disruption
item.description: Countries have so far failed to anchor global traffic compared to Gulf rivals

item.title: Asian start-ups evolve to reshape industries with AI
item.description: Entrepreneurs focus on improving established sectors including logistics, manufacturing and healthcare

item.title: Singapore listings remain scarce despite strong equities performance
item.description: IPO numbers begin to improve, but remain very low compared with Hong Kong

item.title: EU trade deal set to change India’s wine industry
item.description: Domestic producers, already under pressure from overcapacity despite a growing market, are adapting ahead of tariff cuts

item.title: From bingo to Melbourne flower show: Humanitix uses ticket fees as ‘engine for good’
item.description: Australian non-profit takes its business model overseas, expanding to the UK and North America

item.title: The biggest threat to China’s global champions is not Trump
item.description: The most competitive companies are being damaged by an underclass of less productive domestic rivals

item.title: China lures home its top AI talent from Silicon Valley
item.description: Engineers and scientists return for better pay and quality of life as US grows more hostile

item.title: Venezuela passes mining reform law as it seeks private investment
item.description: Corruption and violence in mineral-rich regions likely to pose barrier to foreign companies

item.title: FirstFT: Asian refineries rush to secure oil cargoes as Iran keeps hold over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: 7-Eleven owner delays US listing and CLSA curtails flagship Tokyo forum

item.title: Italian government moves to oust Leonardo chief executive
item.description: Rome proposes that Roberto Cingolani be succeeded by company’s former chief commercial officer

item.title: xAI sues Colorado over first state AI anti-discrimination law
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI lab claims the regulations violate free speech protections

item.title: North Sea oil prices hit record high as Iran keeps hold over Hormuz
item.description: Washington-Tehran ceasefire agreement fails to stem global energy crunch

item.title: For Oura and Whoop, health has a reasonable chance of being wealth
item.description: The two health technology wearable companies have recently raised money at valuations of $11bn and $10bn respectively

item.title: A spokesperson could not be reached for comment (possibly because she doesn’t exist)
item.description: The curious case of Emilia Carrière, alleged comms professional

item.title: Elon Musk’s grip on the future of US space exploration
item.description: As SpaceX gears up for an IPO, America should avoid becoming over-reliant

item.title: London mayor takes aim at social media companies over disinformation
item.description: Research published by City Hall found accounts aligned with the extreme right are among those smearing the capital online

item.title: The quantum computing race is wide open
item.description: Investor money has been flowing into a widening range of companies with differing technologies

item.title: Bill Ackman tries to make music and money mix with €55bn Universal bid
item.description: Billionaire hedge fund manager’s pursuit reignites debate about ‘financialisation’ of music industry

item.title: Chinese carmakers double share of UK market in March
item.description: Surge in interest in electric vehicles following the outbreak of Iran war boosts Chinese brands

item.title: The chips chokehold that could end the AI investment boom
item.description: Taiwan’s control of leading-edge silicon chips lies on a geostrategic faultline

item.title: EU urged to create €10bn-€65bn fund for natural and climate disasters
item.description: Insurance gap grows as only ‘a fraction’ of losses are covered

item.title: Unilever buys vitamin gummies brand Grüns in wellness push
item.description: Group expands wellbeing division on back of supplements boom after agreeing to sell its food arm

item.title: Uzbekistan prepares to list state assets in London
item.description: Move by National Investment Fund marks country’s first foray into global stock markets

item.title: Listen up: AI’s communication blind spot
item.description: How we hear other humans is more crucial than ever

item.title: Adidas set to lose Champions League match ball contract to Nike after 25 years
item.description: Deal for top three competitions is expected to rise sharply from current value and could exceed €40mn a year

item.title: UK exposes covert Russian submarine operation
item.description: Defence secretary says mission was monitored ‘24-7’ by British warships

item.title: EU to relax methane rules to secure energy supplies
item.description: Proposed changes offer ‘flexibilities’ to strict requirements due to be imposed on fossil fuel importers

item.title: OpenAI halts Stargate UK data centre project
item.description: Company blames high energy costs and regulatory uncertainty in blow to Starmer’s effort to build ‘sovereign’ AI

item.title: The AI job loss story is all about bundles
item.description: Evidence on white-collar work displacement is beginning to match the theory
